originalTitle: Nigerian retired general abducted with his wife in the north-west
title: A Brave General and Staying Safe in Nigeria!
content:
Hello from Africa! Your Friendly News Reporter Here!

Hello everyone, my super special friends! It's your friendly news reporter, Sunny, here to share some important news with you from our amazing continent, Africa! Today, we are going to talk about something that happened in a big, beautiful country called Nigeria. Nigeria is one of our biggest countries in Africa, full of wonderful people, incredible music, and delicious food! We want everyone to be safe and happy, always. Sometimes, things happen that make us think about how important it is to keep everyone safe. Let's learn together, in a simple way.

Meet General Rabe and His Wife!

Our story today is about a kind man named Major General Rabe Abubakar. A "General" is a very important leader in the army. The army is like a big team of brave people who help protect everyone in a country. General Rabe was a very important person who worked hard to keep Nigeria safe for many, many years. He is now retired, which means he finished his big job and was enjoying his time. He lives with his dear wife, who is also a lovely person. They are a happy family, just like your family! Did you know? Nigeria has more than 200 million people! That's a lot of friends! It's the country with the most people in all of Africa.

What Happened?

One day, something not-so-good happened. General Rabe and his wife were at their home, just minding their own business. Then, some people came and took them away. We don't know why, and it's a sad thing when people are taken without their permission. This happened in a part of Nigeria called the north-west. When someone important like a General goes missing, everyone gets very worried. Their families miss them, and all the people in the country hope they come back home very soon. It’s like when your friend goes missing from the playground for a little while, and everyone looks for them. But this was more serious. Did you know? The north-west of Nigeria is known for its beautiful ancient cities and traditions. It's a place with a rich history!

Why is Safety Important?

This story reminds us about something super important: safety! Everyone, big or small, wants to feel safe. Feeling safe means you can play, learn, and sleep without being scared. When General Rabe and his wife were taken, it showed that sometimes, even in a big, happy country like Nigeria, there can be "security challenges." This big word just means that some people are not always kind, and they do things that make others feel unsafe. It means we need strong rules and brave people to help keep everyone protected. Did you know? Always tell a trusted adult if you ever feel unsafe or if you see something that makes you worried! They are there to help you.

Our Beautiful Nigeria!

Nigeria is a land of wonder! It has big, bustling cities like Lagos, and calm, green villages. There are many different tribes and languages, all living together. Imagine a country with so many different types of food, music, and dances! It truly is a place of amazing diversity. Even with its beauty, sometimes there are problems, just like any big family can have problems. The leaders and the people of Nigeria are working very hard to solve these problems and make sure everyone can live peacefully. Did you know? Nigeria is famous for its Nollywood movies, which is one of the biggest film industries in the world, just like Hollywood!

How We Stay Safe Together

When things like this happen, it shows how important it is for everyone to work together. The police, the army, and community leaders all have a big job. They try their best to find people who are missing and to stop bad things from happening. But it's not just them! Every person in Nigeria, and all over Africa, can help. We can be kind to each other, look out for our neighbors, and report anything strange to a grown-up we trust. When we all care for each other, our communities become stronger and safer. We are like a big, helpful team!

Working for Peace and Happiness!

The good news is that people are always working very hard to make sure everyone in Nigeria is safe and happy. When problems like this happen, it makes everyone think about how we can make our homes and our country even safer. It reminds us that our soldiers and police are very brave, always trying their best. We hope General Rabe and his wife are found safe and sound very, very soon. Let’s all send our good wishes to them and to everyone in Nigeria, wishing them peace and safety! Remember, together we are strong! Did you know? Many children in Nigeria go to school every day, just like you, learning and growing up to be important people! Education helps make our communities even better.
summary: General Rabe Abubakar and his wife, important people who helped Nigeria, were taken from their home, which reminds us all about how important it is for everyone to be safe and for communities to work together.
whyMatters: It's important because it shows that even in wonderful countries like Nigeria, we need to make sure everyone feels safe, and it teaches us that grown-ups and communities are working hard to protect each other.

title: Ethiopia is heading to the polls, but not everyone can vote
content: Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed's party is expected to dominate the election as conflicts overshadow the vote.

originalTitle: Juan Manuel Santos on Colombia’s peace process, 10 years on
title: A Story of Peace from Far Away Colombia!
content:
Hello, Friends! Reporter Kiki is Here!

Hello, amazing friends! Your reporter Kiki is here to share a very special story with you today! It comes all the way from a beautiful country called Colombia. Colombia is a land of vibrant colours, tall mountains, and green forests, located far away on a big continent called South America. Our story is about a kind and very brave man named Mr. Juan Manuel Santos. Mr. Santos used to be the *president* of Colombia. Do you know what a president does? A president is like the main leader or the 'head teacher' of a whole country! They help make big decisions for everyone who lives there, just like your head teacher makes decisions for your school. Mr. Santos worked incredibly hard to bring something super important to his country: *peace*! Peace means no fighting, no arguments, just everyone getting along, being safe, and happy. Isn't that a wonderful idea? It means friends playing together and families feeling safe in their homes.

A Big Problem in Colombia

For a very, very long time, Colombia had a big, big problem. There was a lot of *fighting* and sadness in the country. Different groups of people were not getting along, and they were hurting each other. This kind of fighting, where people don't feel safe and there's trouble, is called *conflict* or *violence*. Imagine if your school playground had fighting every single day. It would be very sad and scary, right? You wouldn't want to go to school or play outside. That's how it was for many, many years in Colombia. Families were worried all the time, and children couldn't always play safely or go to school without fear. *Did you know?* This big fighting in Colombia went on for more than 50 years! That's a super long time! It's even longer than your great-grandparents have been alive! Imagine that many years of people feeling unsafe. It was a very hard time for many.

Working Towards Peace

Mr. Santos knew in his heart that this fighting had to stop. He wanted all the people in Colombia to live safely and happily together. He wanted children to be able to play and learn without being scared. So, he decided to start something very brave and very important. He started a *peace process*. What is a peace process? It's like when you and your friend have a big disagreement or an argument. Instead of shouting or being angry at each other, you both decide to sit down and talk. You listen to each other's feelings, and you try to understand why your friend is upset, and your friend tries to understand you. Then, you find a way to be friends again. That's what a peace process is for a whole country! It means bringing all the fighting groups together to talk, listen, and find a way to stop fighting for good. It wasn't easy at all. It took many, many long talks and a lot of courage and patience from everyone involved to keep trying, even when it was difficult.

A Wonderful Peace Deal!

Guess what happened after all that hard work and all those long talks? Mr. Santos and all the brave people involved did it! They talked and talked, and finally, they made a big *peace deal*! A peace deal is like a special promise. It's a promise that everyone will stop fighting and will try their best to live together nicely and peacefully, following new rules to help everyone get along. This was a huge, incredibly happy day for Colombia! People all over the country celebrated with joy. They felt hopeful and excited for a future with no more fighting, where they could live without fear. Families could start to rebuild their lives, and children could look forward to playing safely and going to school. *Did you know?* Because Mr. Santos worked so, so hard and was so brave to bring peace to his country, he won a super special award! It's called the Nobel Peace Prize. It's one of the most important prizes in the world, given to people who do amazing things to help bring peace to the world! How cool is that to be recognized for such good work?

Challenges on the Road to Lasting Peace

So, after such a wonderful peace deal and big celebrations, did everything become perfect right away? Well, not always completely perfect. Sometimes, even after a big promise, keeping peace can still be tricky and needs constant effort from everyone. It's like needing to water a plant every day to help it grow big and strong. Most people in Colombia stopped fighting, which was wonderful and made many, many people happy! But sadly, in some smaller parts of the country, a little bit of *violence* came back. This means that some smaller groups of people started fighting again, even after the big peace promise was made. It's a bit like when you make up with a friend after an argument, but then sometimes you might have a tiny disagreement again later. This makes Mr. Santos and many other people who love peace feel a bit sad. They want the peace to last forever and for everyone to keep their promise, all the time. They know that building true, lasting peace is a journey that continues, not just a one-time event. It takes a lot of care, understanding, and talking to make sure peace grows strong everywhere.

Why Peace is Super Important!

So, what does this amazing story from far-away Colombia teach all of us, even kids like you in Africa? It teaches us that *peace is one of the most important things in the whole wide world*. Peace makes everyone safe and allows them to dream big dreams for their future. It teaches us that even when things are very, very hard, we should always, always try to talk and make friends instead of fighting. Fighting makes everyone sad, scared, and hurts people. But talking, listening, and sharing make everyone happy, safe, and helps everyone grow and build wonderful things together. Mr. Santos is still thinking and talking about how to help Colombia stay peaceful and make sure the peace promise is kept by everyone, everywhere. He hopes that everyone will remember how important that big promise of peace was, and how precious it is. It reminds all of us that peace is a precious gift. It's a gift we must all work together to keep safe and strong every single day, in our homes, at our schools, and in our countries.

What Can YOU Do?

Even kids like you, no matter where you live, can help make the world a more peaceful place! How? By being kind to your friends, by sharing your toys, and by using your words to solve problems instead of fighting. When someone is sad, you can offer a hug. When someone needs help, you can offer your hand. These small acts of kindness help build peace right in your own home and at your school. Every time you choose kindness, you are a peacemaker! You are helping to make the world a better, safer, and happier place for everyone. *Did you know?* When countries are peaceful, kids can go to school safely, families can live happily together, and everyone can build a better future with more food, better homes, and lots of fun and games! Peace helps everything good in the world grow strong and beautiful, just like a garden with lots of sunshine! Let's all be like Mr. Santos and be peacemakers every day! Hip, hip, hooray for peace!
summary: A former leader of Colombia, Mr. Juan Manuel Santos, worked very hard to stop fighting in his country and make a peace deal, even though keeping peace is sometimes tricky and needs constant effort.
whyMatters: This story matters because it teaches us that stopping fighting and bringing peace is very important for everyone to be safe and happy, and that we should always try to talk and be kind, just like Mr. Santos did, to make the world a better place.
